question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user set retry.minBackoff to its minimum (100ms) and retry.maxBackoff to its minimum (500ms) via an exposed configuration endpoint, under the precondition that a crafted stream file causes repeated parse failures, causing the retry loop to spin at maximum speed and saturate CPU/DB resources, preventing any other transaction files from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getStreamType() to return a StreamType that does not match the actual stream being processed by exploiting a misconfiguration injection, under the precondition that the StreamFileHealthIndicator uses getStreamType() to tag metrics and a mismatch causes the wrong timer to be looked up, causing the health indicator to always return UNKNOWN and masking parser failures that halt transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a thread starvation condition by setting frequency to 0 (if validation is bypassed via direct field injection), under the precondition that the polling loop uses frequency as a sleep duration and a zero duration causes a busy-wait loop, consuming 100% CPU and starving the parser worker threads from processing actual transactions?
